title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat, but its widespread use may lead to potential risks if the tool contains unvetted code, introduces false positives/negatives, or is exploited by malicious actors to identify vulnerabilities in systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before integrating it into your environment.
Use the tool in a controlled environment to verify its accuracy and reliability.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool and stay informed about any reported vulnerabilities in the tool itself.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. This vulnerability affects the function rt.ReloadConfig of the file pkg/channels/pico/pico.go. Performing a manipulation of the argument message.send results in missing authorization. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The reported GitHub issue was closed automatically due to inactivity.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15320.

title: A weakness has been identified in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. Affected by this issue is some...
description: A weakness has been identified in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file pkg/channels/mqtt/mqtt.go of the component MQTT Channel Handler. This manipulation of the argument client_id causes incorrect authorization. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The reported GitHub issue was closed automatically due to inactivity.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in Sipeed PicoClaw up to version 0.2.9 allows for incorrect authorization via manipulation of the client_id argument in the MQTT Channel Handler. This could enable unauthorized access or privilege escalation, potentially compromising the integrity of systems relying on this component. The public availability of the exploit increases the risk of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Sipeed PicoClaw as soon as it becomes available or implement a temporary workaround to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to MQTT channels by implementing strict authentication and authorization controls, such as client certificate validation.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts, and apply network segmentation to limit the impact of potential exploitation.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. This affects the...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. This affects the function IPAllowlist of the file web/backend/middleware/access_control.go of the component Launcher. Such manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The name of the patch is 3126. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15319.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. Affected by this...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Sipeed PicoClaw up to 0.2.9. Affected by this vulnerability is the function WebFetchTool.Execute of the file pkg/tools/integration/web.go of the component Guarded Web Fetch Flow. The manipulation results in server-side request forgery. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The reported GitHub issue was closed automatically due to inactivity.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15317.

title: A vulnerability was identified in NousResearch hermes-agent up to 2026.5.29.2. Affected by this...
description: A vulnerability was identified in NousResearch hermes-agent up to 2026.5.29.2. Affected by this issue is the function MatrixAdapter._markdown_to_html of the file gateway/platforms/matrix.py of the component Matrix Adapter. Such manipulation leads to cross site scripting. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The pull request to fix this issue awaits acceptance.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15311.

title: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authentication bypass in versions up to...
description: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authentication bypass in versions up to and including 6.2.3 via the Spotify Social Login addon. This is due to the loginpress_on_spotify_login() function trusting the unverified 'email' field returned by Spotify's /v1/me endpoint and using it directly with get_user_by('email', $profile['email']) to identify and log in an existing WordPress account, without confirming that the Spotify user actually owns the email address (Spotify documents

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to WordPress accounts, potentially leading to data breaches, defacement, or further exploitation of compromised websites.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the LoginPress Pro plugin to the latest patched version if available.
Disable the Spotify Social Login addon temporarily until a fix is confirmed.
Audit user accounts for unauthorized access and monitor logs for suspicious login activity.

title: Cotonti Siena 0.9.26 and earlier contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows...
description: Cotonti Siena 0.9.26 and earlier contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated users with PFS access to inject arbitrary script payloads by supplying malicious HTML in the ntitle parameter processed through the TXT filter in pfs.main.php. Attackers can create a folder with a crafted title containing script tags that are stored unescaped in the database and execute in the browser of any user who views the folder listing, including administrators.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58144.

title: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via the GitHub...
description: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via the GitHub OAuth callback in versions up to, and including, 6.2.3. The vulnerability exists in the loginpress_on_github_login() function, which blindly trusts the first element (profile[0]['email']) of the array returned by GitHub's /user/emails endpoint as an account-binding identifier without verifying that the email carries a verified === true status. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to log

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the LoginPress Pro plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to WordPress accounts. This could lead to data breaches, website defacement, or unauthorized administrative actions.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the LoginPress Pro plugin to a patched version if available. If no patch is available, temporarily disable the GitHub OAuth login functionality.
Review and monitor user accounts for any unauthorized access or suspicious activity, especially those using GitHub OAuth for authentication.
Implement additional security measures such as two-factor authentication (2FA) to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access.

title: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via Unverified...
description: The LoginPress Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass via Unverified OAuth Email in all versions up to and including 6.2.3. The vulnerability exists in the loginpress_on_discord_login() Discord OAuth callback handler, which accepts the email field returned by Discord's /users/@me endpoint without ever checking that the profile's verified flag is true, then directly maps that email to a local WordPress account via get_user_by('email', $profile['email']) and issues an auth

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to WordPress accounts by exploiting unverified email addresses returned by Discord's OAuth endpoint. This could lead to account compromise, data breaches, privilege escalation, and potential website defacement or malicious activity.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the LoginPress Pro plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily if no fix exists.
Implement additional verification mechanisms for OAuth logins, such as checking the 'verified' flag in the OAuth response.
Monitor access logs for suspicious login activity and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all WordPress accounts.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.788 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability that...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.788 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary shell commands by accessing the embedded terminal API endpoints without credentials. Attackers can create a session, attach a PTY shell, and write arbitrary commands through the terminal input endpoint to achieve full command execution as the server process user via four sequential unauthenticated HTTP requests.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58123.

title: An Improper Handling of Undefined Parameters vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (pfe)...
description:
An Improper Handling of Undefined Parameters vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (pfe) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on EX Series devices allows an authenticated attacker with low privileges to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS).

If an attempt is made to subscribe to an unsupported telemetry sensor path on EX2300, EX3400, EX4000, EX4100 and EX4400 via gRPC, this causes the FPC to crash. This leads to a complete service outage until the module has automatically restarted. 

The following l

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57032.

title: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.307 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows...
description: Hermes WebUI before 0.51.307 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to circumvent local-origin IP restrictions on onboarding endpoints by supplying a spoofed X-Forwarded-For header with a loopback address. Attackers can exploit this bypass to perform server-side request forgery against internal services including cloud metadata endpoints, overwrite LLM provider configuration and API keys with attacker-controlled values, or initiate OAuth devi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The authentication bypass vulnerability in Hermes WebUI before version 0.51.307 allows unauthenticated attackers to exploit local-origin IP restrictions via a spoofed X-Forwarded-For header. This can lead to server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks against internal services, compromise of sensitive cloud metadata, unauthorized modification of LLM provider configurations, and exposure of API keys. These actions could result in data breaches, service disruptions, and unauthorized access to critical resources.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Hermes WebUI to version 0.51.307 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict validation of the X-Forwarded-For header to prevent spoofing attempts.
Conduct a thorough review of internal services and configurations to identify and mitigate potential SSRF risks.

title: Cotonti Siena 0.9.26 and earlier contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows...
description: Cotonti Siena 0.9.26 and earlier contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to modify administrator configuration by tricking a logged-in administrator into submitting a forged POST request to the admin.php config update handler, which never invokes the application's CSRF validation function. Attackers can disable the PFS module's file extension whitelist by setting pfsfilecheck to 0, enabling any user with PFS access to upload and execute arbitrary

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-58143.

title: An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the packet forwarding...
description:
An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (PFE) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series allows adjacent subscribers to bypass configured firewall filters.

On MX Series devices with MPC10/11, LC4800/9600, and MX304 with subscribers configured on static interfaces, ingress firewall filters are not enforced, so that neither protocol level nor upstream bandwidth limitation are in effect. 


This issue affects Junos OS on MX with MPC10/11

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows adjacent subscribers to bypass configured firewall filters on affected Juniper Networks MX Series devices, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or denial of service due to the lack of protocol-level and bandwidth enforcement.
affected_industries: Enterprises using Juniper MX Series devices
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates provided by Juniper Networks to address CVE-2026-57031.
Implement additional network segmentation and monitoring to detect and mitigate unauthorized access or traffic anomalies.
Review and update firewall configurations and access control lists (ACLs) to minimize exposure to adjacent subscribers.

title: A Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference vulnerability in the URL filtering plugin of...
description:
A Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference vulnerability in the URL filtering plugin of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to bypass web filtering and access downstream resources that should be unreachable.



If an MX Series device is configured with web filtering, and an attacker sends a request with a specifically formatted URL, this request will get forwarded despite the system being configured to block it. In turn, an attacker can a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57054.

title: An Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input vulnerability in the SIP plugin of...
description:
An Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input vulnerability in the SIP plugin of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series with SPC3 and SRX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS).If the SIP ALG is enabled on an affected device, the processing of a malformed SIP invite packet will cause a flow processing daemon (flowd) crash and restart. This leads to a complete service outage until the system has automatically recovered.



This issue

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57026.

title: A Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')...
description:
A Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition') vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (PFE) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on SRX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS).

As part of the stateful traffic processing on SRX Series devices flows are being established, and removed when not needed anymore. During the removal process the timeout of a flow should be set to 3 seconds and consequential

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The race condition vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine of Juniper Networks Junos OS on SRX Series devices could allow an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS), potentially disrupting network traffic and critical operations.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and updates provided by Juniper Networks to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation and access controls to limit exposure to untrusted networks.
Monitor network traffic for unusual patterns or signs of exploitation and establish an incident response plan.

title: An Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input vulnerability in the TCP proxy plugin of...
description:
An Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input vulnerability in the TCP proxy plugin of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series with SPC3, and SRX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause a complete Denial of Service (DoS).

When TCP proxy is engaged in a flow session, to support ALGs, Advanced Anti-Malware, ICAP or UTM, a TCP packet with specifically malformed TCP header will cause flow processing daemon (flowd) to crash and restart. This causes a complete service

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57023.

title: A Missing Synchronization vulnerability in the flow collector handler of Juniper Networks Junos...
description:
A Missing Synchronization vulnerability in the flow collector handler of Juniper Networks Junos OS Evolved on QFX Series allows an adjacent, unauthenticated attacker to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS).


When the reachability of an sFlow collector changes, the corresponding next-hop entry is updated. If this update occurs simultaneously with the sFlow thread accessing the next-hop data (which is outside the attackers control), it causes the evo-pfemand process to crash, impacting all traffic for

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57029.

title: An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the Packet Forwarding...
description:
An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the Packet Forwarding Engine (PFE) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX with SPC3 and SRX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS).

When an affected device initiates a TCP connection to an attacker-controlled system that responds with a specific packet, this causes a PFE crash and restart, which affects all services until the system has automatically recovered.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57022.

title: CVE-2026-13722 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the
description: WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a firmware validation bypass when processing a backup image via the backup/restore feature. An authenticated administrator can exploit this vulnerability to install a tampered firmware image.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2025.6.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows an authenticated administrator to bypass firmware validation and install tampered firmware images, potentially leading to unauthorized control of the device, data breaches, or disruption of network operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update WatchGuard Fireware OS to a patched version that addresses CVE-2026-13722.
Restrict administrative access to trusted users and networks, and enforce strong authentication mechanisms.
Regularly monitor and validate firmware integrity to detect unauthorized modifications.

title: CVE-2026-13384 - An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS wgagent process could allow an authen
description: An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS wgagent process could allow an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted requests to the Management Web UI.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 12.1 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13384.

title: CVE-2026-13383 - An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS ikestubd process could allow an authe
description: An Out-of-bounds Write vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS ikestubd process could allow an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted requests to the Management Web UI.This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 12.1 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated privileged user to execute arbitrary code on affected WatchGuard Fireware OS devices, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, or disruption of network operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by WatchGuard to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the Management Web UI to trusted IP addresses and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.
Monitor system logs for any suspicious activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting the ikestubd process.

title: CVE-2026-13377 - Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerab
description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS SIP Proxy module allows Stored XSS. This vulnerability is an additional unmitigated attack path for CVE-2025-6947.

This issue affects Fireware OS 12.0 up to and including 12.12, 12.5 up to and including 12.5.18, and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13377.

description:

title: CVE-2026-13371 - An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management
description: An authenticated administrator can trigger a denial-of-service condition in the Fireware Management Web UI by sending malformed or crafted data to the put_data endpoint, which performs unsafe deserialization of the attacker-supplied input.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13371.

title: CVE-2026-13368 - WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP a
description:
WatchGuard Fireware OS contains a race condition leading to a use-after-free vulnerability in LDAP authentication for the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2. A remote unauthenticated attacker could exploit this vulnerability to execute arbitrary code in the context of the iked process on Fireboxes that have a Mobile VPN with IKEv2 configured to use an external LDAP authentication server.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13368.

title: CVE-2026-13084 - A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticate
description:
A null pointer dereference vulnerability in WatchGuard Fireware OS may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition by sending specially crafted IKEv2 messages. This vulnerability affects both the Mobile User VPN with IKEv2 and the Branch Office VPN using IKEv2 when configured with a dynamic gateway peer.

This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.10.2 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13084.

title: CVE-2026-13079 - A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows
description:
A local privilege escalation vulnerability in the WatchGuard Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows allows a local attacker to escalate their privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM on the machine where the client is installed.

This issue affects the Mobile VPN with SSL client for Windows up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13079.

title: CVE-2026-13054 - A path traversal vulnerability in the WatchGuard Fireware OS Management Web UI allows a privileged a
description:
A path traversal vulnerability in the WatchGuard Fireware OS Management Web UI allows a privileged authenticated attacker to write arbitrary files on the Firebox's filesystem.




This vulnerability affects Fireware OS 11.0 up to and including 11.12.4_Update1, 12.0 up to and including 12.12 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13054.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13053.
